summary: ACCEPT. Final R3 review verifies the fresh desktop-only CI repair and preserves the original host-hash canary guarantees. With generated apps/web/dist removed, pnpm desktop:security-check and pnpm desktop:package both pass and copy-renderer logs an explicit optional web-renderer skip. After pnpm build:web, pnpm desktop:package copies the canary web renderer into renderer/web, and fresh Electron plus Chromium host-hash canary commands match the Node baseline for hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, and save byte length 10920. Electron security remains narrow and no CI workflow, root package, lockfile, web package dependency, or production dependency change was found.

tests_run.result: On test/m2-host-hash-canary. Dirty paths are intended canary changes, allowed lead-owned orchestration/task files, route files, and review/handoff artifacts.
Tracked source diffs are confined to apps/desktop, apps/sim-runner, apps/web, and allowed project orchestration/task files. No forbidden task path was present.

tests_run.command: Remove generated apps/web/dist after verifying resolved path is inside workspace
tests_run.result: Removed generated web dist: D:/WebProjects/MonsoonSovereigns/apps/web/dist; apps/web/dist existed after removal: False.

Fresh desktop-only path passed with apps/web/dist absent. copy-renderer logged: Skipped optional web renderer assets; D:/WebProjects/MonsoonSovereigns/apps/web/dist does not exist. Electron preferences passed; preload exposes only monsoonDesktop; renderer navigation is restricted; IPC allowlist remains desktop:getRuntimeInfo and desktop:setFullscreen.
Fresh desktop-only package path passed with apps/web/dist absent. copy-renderer logged the optional skip and packaged main, preload, renderer, and platform adapter artifacts.
Vite built dist/index.html, dist/host-hash-canary.html, the host-hash-canary worker bundle, and shared assets; only the existing chunk-size warning was emitted.

Canary-present package path passed after pnpm build:web. copy-renderer copied web renderer assets to apps/desktop/dist/renderer/web, then packaged desktop artifacts to .tmp/desktop-package/MonsoonSovereigns-win32-x64.
All three canary-present artifact checks returned True.
Packaged Electron renderer Dedicated Worker matched Node baseline: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save byte length 10920.
Chromium Dedicated Worker matched Node baseline: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save byte length 10920.
Root check passed: Prettier format check, ESLint, recursive workspace typecheck, and architecture boundary check all completed successfully.
Node and Worker-compatible hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save byte length 10920.

tests_run.command: git diff --name-status -- .github package.json pnpm-lock.yaml apps/web/package.json apps/sim-runner/package.json
tests_run.result: Only apps/sim-runner/package.json changed, adding the sim:host-hash-canary script. No CI workflow, root package, lockfile, or apps/web package dependency diff was present.
R3 writer handoff validates against project/schemas/handoff.schema.json.
Task JSON shape ok: M2-HOST-HASH-CANARY-001 IN_PROGRESS 6 acceptance criteria and 10 required tests.

tests_run.command: rg static security and worker scans
tests_run.result: Confirmed copy-renderer optional skip/copy behavior, canary source uses a real new Worker(..., { type: 'module' }) path, Electron main gates canary load behind MONSOON_HOST_HASH_CANARY=1, and security settings remain nodeIntegration=false, contextIsolation=true, sandbox=true, webSecurity=true.

decisions: Accept M2-HOST-HASH-CANARY-001 after R3 because the fresh desktop-only CI failure mode is repaired and all original host-hash acceptance criteria remain satisfied.
apps/web/dist is now an optional generated input for ordinary desktop security/package jobs; absence no longer fails desktop-only CI.
The canary-present path remains explicit: run pnpm build:web before pnpm desktop:package, then the packaged Electron renderer/Worker host canary is available and matches the Node baseline.
Electron security posture is unchanged: no new IPC channel, no preload bridge expansion, no Node integration, no remote URL loading, and no filesystem authority was introduced for the canary.
No CI workflow files, lockfile, root package manifest, apps/web package manifest, or production dependencies were changed.
risks: The packaged Electron canary page is intentionally absent from desktop-only packages when apps/web/dist has not been built; this is acceptable for normal desktop security/package CI and is covered by the explicit build:web -> desktop:package canary sequence.

The task JSON currently records status IN_PROGRESS while the routed handoff is REVIEW. taskctl validation passes and the R3 route is explicit, so this is treated as lead-owned coordination state to reconcile during acceptance integration rather than a task blocker.
